description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Home and away teams, date and time, season type and week etc. are included. Also includes gameday information. This includes full stadium information (capacity, lat/long, surface etc.), top-line betting information (spread, moneyline, total), weather conditions, and broadcast information.

Examples: 2018, 2018PRE, 2018POST, 2018STAR, 2019, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns the non-full-game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games on a given date. Non-full-game means 1st-half or 1st-quarter, for example, rather than full game. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

The list of current BettingEvents for the given date. Events in this include market information but no outcomes will be included here. Intended to allow both visibility to Events in order to match up Events -> Games via the included GameID (where applicable) as well as provide a list of MarketIDs which are included in the given event.

Examples: 2020-02-15, 2020-02-23.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Returns the full list of BetttingEvents for the given season. Intended for those who need to tie BettingEventIDs to GameIDs.

Examples: 2020, 2020PRE, 2020POST, 2020STAR, 2021, etc.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season (with optional season type).
Examples: 2020, 2020PRE, 2020POST, 2020STAR, 2021, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given BettingEventID.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets). Valid entries include: 134, 155, etc.
(Required) The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for `Game Lines` 2 for `Player Props` 3 for `Team Props` 6 for `Game Props`

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The GameID of the desired game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets). Valid entries include: 14814

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Describes recent team trends and performance against betting data in recent sets of games.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) The abbreviation of the requested team.
Examples: PHI, MIN, DET, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns trends data for a given pairing of teams. Will return data even if the teams are not set to play this season. Intended for use on a specific game, though it will work for other comparisons if applicable.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) The abbreviation of the requested team.
Examples: PHI, MIN, DET, etc.

(Required) The abbreviation of the requested opponent.
Examples: PHI, MIN, DET, etc.

This works in the same way as Betting Markets by Market Type but is prefiltered to the Player Props type only. Ideal if your application will only ever require Player Props, but if you also want Team Props etc. it is recommended to use the by Market Type endpoint.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The unique GameID of the game in question.

description.content: Returns the full-game and non-full-game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games in a given week and season. Only returns the most recently seen odds, not inclusive of line movement. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

Examples: 2018-06-20, 2018-06-23.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Returns the game odds ( for games on a given date. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Betting Markets - by Market Type [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Betting Markets - by Market Type [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

query.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets). Valid entries include: 134, 155, etc.
(Required) The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for `Game Lines` 2 for `Player Props` 3 for `Team Props` 6 for `Game Props`

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

response.name: Success

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets). Valid entries include: 134, 155, etc.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Returns full line movement for a given BettingMarket. Due to the sheer size of the output and the level of detail, it is intended for historical data purposes and not for the most up-to-the-second lines. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets. Valid entries include: 421, 1041, etc.

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

response.name: Success

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Provide a market ID that supports resulting (i.e. has a ResultType) and this endpoint will return a result: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

Examples: G1100

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games on a given date. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The GameID of an NBA game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 12780 or 12781

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100
(Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The GameID of an NBA game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 12780 or 12781

(Required) The name of the Sportsbook grouping.

item.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

request.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

description.content: Returns the game odds ( for games on a given date. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The GameID of an NBA game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14620, 16905, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Full statistical information for a specified game, down to the team and player stat level, delivered live during the games, called for all games on a given date.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2015-JUL-31, 2015-SEP-01.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2015-JUL-31, 2015-SEP-01.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

This method returns all box scores for a given date, but only returns player stats that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. By definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2015-JUL-31, 2015-SEP-01.
(Required) Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are: 1 or 2.

response.name: Success

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2015-JUL-31, 2015-SEP-01.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.
(Required) The GameID of an NBA game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14620, 16905, etc.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Full statistical information for a specified date for each game that took place, down to the team and player stat level, delivered after the game is complete.

variable.description: (Required) Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

(Required) The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2015-JUL-31, 2015-SEP-01.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

This method returns all play-by-plays for a given season and week, but only returns plays that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. By definition this is a live endpoint, not final.
